steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage offers a well-executed marketing campaign as the specific example of what drives a book's commercial success. The blank names what that campaign generates.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to name what a marketing campaign produces that drives book sales. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — originality: the quality of being new or creative — marketing campaigns don't create originality in a book; they spread awareness of it. ✗
  • Choice B — publicity: public attention and promotion — a marketing campaign is precisely the mechanism for generating publicity, which drives sales. ✓
  • Choice C — complexity: the quality of being intricate — campaigns don't make a book more complex; they make it more visible. ✗
  • Choice D — practicality: usefulness for real-world application — campaigns don't make a book more practical; they promote it. ✗
That matches Choice B — publicity.
